titleStmt.title: Letter from Fr. Martin Mahoney to John Sweetman, 16 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Fr. Martin Mahoney

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Fr. Martin Mahoney to John Sweetman. Mahoney, a parish priest in Minnesota, edited the Catholic weekly 'Manifestos'. Sweetman (1844-1936) was a member of Sinn Féin and patron to Arthur Griffith. In 1915 he spoke out against conscription and was arrested in the aftermath of the 1916 Rising. The letter refers mainly to business and religious matters. It is written on the back of an older, typed letter by Mahoney relating to church collections.

correspAction.placeName: Drumbaragh Estate, Kells, County Meath, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

ab.seg:
Mendota. Minn 2 16 16 Dear Mr. Sweetman: Yours came maybe 2 weeks ago, but
some rush that made set aside the little pamphlet, which
only today did I turn up, & read. And reading it I culled back
all the blame I had planned giving you for the fresh indulgence
of the pamphlet habit. For it is very good, very effective; so much
so that I've enclosed it to the editor of the I.M saying I felt he
too would judge it "good stuff". As to your boy I almost dread to provoke your contrariness
by saying how much better I believe the packing business. Like
fast horses, the devil's due to to'em, University graduates are
already too numerous, and no account, but an Irish Swift
or Adenauer, there will be a man to deserve a national statue!
Only, let a most unbusiness man say it, that splendid scheme
to get a fair show, to get started in modern fashion should have
many interested & involved in it, furnishing the capital, and
the widespread interest, and manifold boosting necessary
not only for its adequate start, but as I conceive for the much
sharper, later trial of from the inevitable efforts of rivals to kill it
& also . Father Condon, now I think in Tramore, once my fellow
assistant in Preston, Eng, amazed me by the account of how a
cotton mill of 5000 operatives at Thomastown, Tipp or Kilkenny,
got bought, undersold and Killed by a combine of Laws,
concerns in that line, & so for years that everso lively plant is
a ruin. There's a matter where the people need a thoro
anti-toxining to be made to "look before & after and
think of what's not", the years of two prices. That would
follow the wily wreckers "cuts" and bargains. What an
infinite lot people can be shown, as we are shown from
the Brothers. Agricult Mill — why is it not more widely advertised
and known, how we may have whole wheat flour at the store
. I'll send herewith some cuttings held over for a good while. Mrs S. ought collect the specially good & helpful things in those.
Leave a help and letters on Irish Music, harp, tuning etc.
Also you might usefully abridge the Rigg showings on crops acreage;
and give a good document on the several better and more paying uses
of barley than to make booze.
Very sincerely,
M Mahoney . Mendoza, Minn Dec 2nd 1915. Dear Sirs: With the report of last Easter Collection, I am enclosing
the little envelope for the Fall collection which ought to come
in next Sunday. This Fall collection serves to meet two of the
largest Parish outlays, the Seminary assessment and the Fuel, both
of which used to be, and are yet in many places two distinct collections
some times kept up for several Sundays. What an awful
punishment it would be of my desire to spare people that unpleasant
music, if anyone were to confine himself for this one Fall collection
to only such an offering as he would feel bound to make for each
of these old style levies. This is a good occasion for a worthy
thanks offering toi the Lord for the bounties of this year. Sincerely,
Martin Mahoney.
Easter 1915 Collection.
5.00 James Scott.
3.00 D. Tousignant.
2.00 J.J. Beaudette, Arthur Dernier, Ed Dupuis, Jos Fee, Mrs Pat
Fee, James McCarthy &Family, Perron.
1.50 Mick Welch, Jms & Mrs Shields.
1.00 Alex Enge, Fred , M Barry, L. Beaudet, .... einier,
Frank Bouthiler, Fred Bouthiler, Mrs Anne Burne, Chas Burns,
Geo Burns, Hugh Burns, James Burns. M. Burns, Wm Callahan.
Alex Charderdelain, Desire Chardelain, Louis Chardelain,
E Coffee, Thos. Connolly, Jne Cunningham, M. Cunningham
Wm, Deslaurier, Mrs Violet Evans, Agnes Fee, Hugh Fee,
James Fee, Wm Fee, Mrs T. Fisher, l. Gosselin, M. Haggerty,
T. Kenneally, Mrs Kennedy, M. Kennedy, Ed Kearns,
Jno Kearns, .Kester, Jos Lebbey, Whos Lebboy, J.M.Liebel,
Dug Lemay, J.Lemay, J..Lomay, J.J.Lemay, Mor Levendro,
J..Lexandre, F.McCarthy, M.O'Connor, ..O'Moill, dolard,
Permxon, Dolph Pollon, Jos Quigley, M Lovan, A. Moran,
M. Kied, H..Shields, Lillian Shields, M. Shields, Jas Slaton,
Felix joisignant, Matt Walsh, E. Walsh, Gus Weber, L. Weber
Mrs Weber .75 T.F. Bagan, Mrs Fred Lecleure. .50 Matt Beaudette, James Burns, Julia Burns, D. Clemens,
Felix Deslaudier, Louis Fisher, P. Hurley, Mrs P Kaufhold,
M. Luer, Emmanuel Letendro, . Lohendie, Mrs. M. Miller,
P. Mooney, J. O'Neill, Kye Perron, Omar Perron, R. Reid,
Mrs Rhos Ryan (R.I.P.), Renie Shields, J.J. Slater,
Thomas Slater, V. Tonsignant, L Vainencourt, Emily Weber,
Mary Weber, J. Williams, P. Williams. .25 Jos. Urvine, Paul ane, Francis Cayan, Carroll Callahan,
Hugh Callahan, Omar Deslaurier, Joe Fee, K Lemay,
Chas Langer, Jno Slater. Plate $4.02 Total $120.27
Feb
17
9AM
1916 Mr. John Sweetman
Drumbaragh, Kells

Agnes Swee
